# The Last Hour

A scientist has vanished, and you have 60 minutes to find out why. **The Last Hour** is a cybersecurity Digital Educational Escape Room (DEER) for 1-2 players. Explore a web of connected puzzles, investigate suspicious clues, crack codes, and escape before time runs out.

## Requirements

The Last Hour runs on desktop Windows, macOS, and Linux systems. Mobile devices are not currently supported.

Bundled downloads are currently provided for Windows x86_64 and Linux x86_64. macOS users need to use the `.jar` files with Java installed.

## How To Start

*Note: Multiplayer does not currently support internet play. All devices must be connected to the same local network.*

Single-player can be played on one device. Multiplayer requires two separate devices; local co-op on a single device is not yet supported.

### Single-Player

#### If you have [Java 25 or newer](https://www.oracle.com/java/technologies/downloads/#java25) installed

* Download `TheLastHour.jar` below.
* Start it with `java -jar TheLastHour.jar`, or double-click it if your system supports launching `.jar` files.

#### If you do not have Java installed

* On Windows, download `TheLastHour-win-x86_64.zip` and extract it locally.
* On Linux, download `TheLastHour-linux-x86_64.tar.gz` and extract it locally.
* On Windows, start `TheLastHour/TheLastHour.exe`.
* On Linux, start `TheLastHour/bin/TheLastHour` from inside the extracted release folder.

### Multiplayer

#### If you have [Java 25 or newer](https://www.oracle.com/java/technologies/downloads/#java25) installed

* Download `TheLastHourServer.jar` below.
* Download `TheLastHourClient.jar` below.
* Make sure both player devices are connected to the same local network.
* On the host device, start `TheLastHourServer.jar`.
* On client devices, start `TheLastHourClient.jar`.
* Enter the IP address and port shown in the host server window.

#### If you do not have Java installed

* On Windows, download `TheLastHour-win-x86_64.zip` and extract it locally.
* On Linux, download `TheLastHour-linux-x86_64.tar.gz` and extract it locally.
* Make sure both player devices are connected to the same local network.
* On the host device, start `TheLastHourServer/TheLastHourServer.exe` on Windows or `TheLastHourServer/bin/TheLastHourServer` on Linux from inside the extracted release folder.
* On client devices, start `TheLastHourClient/TheLastHourClient.exe` on Windows or `TheLastHourClient/bin/TheLastHourClient` on Linux from inside the extracted release folder.
* Enter the IP address and port shown in the host server window.
